title: Watch: Inside Vijay’s ‘Leo’-inspired cafe in Kashmir
description:
Sifar Cafe in Anantnag district, 40 kilometres from Pahalgam in South Kashmir, has become the epicentre of tourists from South India. It is the exact location where the new Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay’s 2023 hit film Leo was shot.
In the week since he assumed the role as Chief Minister, Sifar has seen a 90% spike in tourists from Tamil Nadu. Visitors throng the location, recreating Vijay’s walk. They also order ‘chocolate coffee’, a famous device used in the film to further the plot.

description: An e-rickshaw driver was lifted nearly 50ft and flung 100 metres by storm winds after trying to secure a tin shed. He landed in a sugarcane field with fractures, his miraculous survival captured in a viral video. The incident highlighted the storm's ferocity and the man's narrow escape.

title: A non-smoker, 23-year-old thought her tongue ulcer to be an accidental bite: It was oral cancer
Post-matric scholarship scam: HC raps Punjab over delay in registering FIRs
Check latest petrol, diesel rates in your city and state after Rs 3 increase
description: Fuel prices have surged across India, with petrol now exceeding Rs 110 per litre in several states and diesel nearing Rs 100 in many regions. This hike, averaging Rs 3 per litre, follows sustained pressure from elevated global crude oil prices, exacerbated by the West Asia crisis.

title: 'Torturous for boxers': Drained Nikhat Zareen suffers shock trials exit
description: Two-time world champion Nikhat Zareen faced a shocking defeat in boxing trials, missing out on CWG and Asian Games. She revealed the immense physical and mental strain caused by a chaotic two-week preparation, citing administrative tussles between BFI and SAI as a major factor. Despite the setback, Zareen plans to focus on rebuilding her fitness for the LA Olympics.

title: ‘Dispute over money’: Accused in Delhi bus gangrape case tells cops; what we know so far
description: Delhi Police have apprehended the driver and conductor of a private sleeper bus. They are accused of raping a 30-year-old woman in Nangloi three days ago. The woman stated she boarded the bus late at night. The accused allegedly drove the vehicle to a secluded area and assaulted her. The accused claim a monetary dispute was involved. Investigations are ongoing.

IPL Playoff Scenarios: PBKS slump to 43.8% as GT and RCB tighten playoff grip
description: With playoffs in sight for some and out of reach for others, the IPL race intensifies. Gujarat Titans and Royal Challengers Bangalore are nearing qualification, while Sunrisers Hyderabad are strong contenders. Chennai Super Kings and Rajasthan Royals hold even chances for the top four, as Punjab Kings' hopes dwindle. Kolkata Knight Riders and Delhi Capitals face slim prospects.

title: Centre hikes petrol, diesel prices by Rs 3 per litre
Fuel prices up: Petrol, diesel hiked by Rs 3/litre amid global crude surge
description: The Centre has repeatedly asserted that there is no fuel shortage in the country and no plan to introduce rationing of petrol, diesel or LPG despite disruptions in global energy shipments linked to the Iran conflict and the Strait of Hormuz crisis.

RPI(Athavale) leader stabbed to death at Pune bar
description: Republican Party of India (Athavale)'s Kaamgar Aghadi state executive president Anil Maruti More was brutally stabbed to death by two assailants at a Moshi bar. The murder is believed to be a fallout of personal enmity, with police investigating CCTV footage and other clues to apprehend the suspects.

Groom not the man she met on Instagram, bride calls off wedding in UP
description: A wedding in Shahabad descended into chaos when the bride alleged the groom was not the man she knew online. The man, identified as Devendra Singh Parmar, claimed he was the one the bride had been speaking to. Police have registered an FIR against 10 people for cheating and conspiracy, arresting five, including the groom.

title: Four charred to death after bus-tanker collision in Surendranagar
Youth late to open Ahmedabad PG facility door killed by roommates
description: A fatal dispute over a room door at a Vejalpur PG accommodation has claimed the life of 25-year-old Hiren Solanki. Two co-residents allegedly stabbed him after a heated argument escalated into a physical altercation. This incident marks the fifth murder in Ahmedabad in just four days, with police swiftly arresting the accused.

title: Sinhagad College of Engineering: Watchman dies by suicide, cites non-payment of salary
CBSE Class 12 results: 16% fall in students scoring 90% or above; on-screen marking in focus
BRICS consensus on West Asia uncertain as Iran, UAE spar at meeting
description: BRICS foreign ministers are struggling to agree on a joint statement concerning West Asia. Tensions flared between the UAE and Iran during the meeting. Iran accused the UAE of direct involvement in aggression. India's External Affairs Minister stressed the importance of consensus for new members. The outcome of the meeting remains uncertain.

title: 117 killed as storms lash UP with cyclone-like 130kmph winds
description: A severe thunderstorm and squall ravaged Uttar Pradesh on Wednesday, claiming 117 lives and injuring 79. Violent storms, collapsing structures, and lightning strikes caused widespread destruction, with Prayagraj being the worst-hit district. High wind speeds, reaching nearly cyclonic levels, uprooted trees and damaged hundreds of houses and livestock.

title: Man held for cheating, raping women after marriage promise: Police

2 men rape orphaned girls aged 11 and 12 for over a year in Bengaluru, post videos online
description: Two men have been arrested in Bengaluru for sexually abusing two minor girls, recording the acts, and uploading them online for profit. The arrests followed an alert from the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal.

title: NIA reveals Pakistan's plan to push 'Ghazwa-e-Hind' through 'doctor-terror' module
description: NIA's chargesheet reveals Ansar Ghazwat-ul-Hind (AGuH) has evolved from a forest-based group to an urban threat, orchestrated by Pakistan-based handlers. This shift, moving from visual propaganda to a tech-driven network, aims to link Kashmiri militancy with global jihadist goals, bypassing traditional structures through encrypted platforms.

description: The complex tensions in Manipur have surged beyond the Meitei and Kuki-Zo conflict, drawing the Naga groups into a heated fray. Retired Lt Gen Prabin Kalita posits that external players may be exploiting the turmoil to further engage the Nagas, intensifying an already volatile situation.

Meta's highest-paid employee is not happy with 'just money motivated' tag for his team
description: Meta SuperIntelligence Lab chief Alexandr Wang is pushing back on the idea that his team joined for the money. On the Core Memory podcast, Wang said the real draw was compute access, talent density, and research freedom—not the $100 million packages Meta reportedly offered. He also addressed the viral soup story involving Mark Zuckerberg and an OpenAI recruit.
